faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants in my Pelham Gardens home?
faqs.answer: Signs of carpenter ants include piles of wood shavings (frass) near baseboards or windowsills, rustling sounds inside walls, and sightings of large black ants especially in spring. In Pelham Gardens' older Tudor and brick homes, check wooden porches, window frames, and areas where moisture may accumulate. Carpenter ants are typically active at night and are larger than regular ants, measuring up to half an inch long.

faqs.question: Are carpenter ants common in Pelham Gardens, Bronx?
faqs.answer: Yes, carpenter ants are quite common in Pelham Gardens due to the neighborhood's mature trees, wooden architectural details in Tudor-style homes, and humid conditions near Pelham Parkway. The area's mix of pre-war and post-war construction with wooden elements provides ideal nesting sites. Properties near wooded areas or with moisture issues from aging plumbing are particularly susceptible to carpenter ant infestations.
